sourceMovie.title: Dallas Buyers Club
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Dallas Buyers Club for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a history rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jean-Marc Vallée keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Richard Jewell
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dallas Buyers Club clicked for you, Richard Jewell (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Ed Wood
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Dallas Buyers Club clicked for you, Ed Wood (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Dallas Buyers Club?
faq.answer: Try Richard Jewell, Ed Wood, The Devils—each shares DNA with Dallas Buyers Club while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Dallas Buyers Club?
faq.answer: Dallas Buyers Club (2013) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Dallas Buyers Club worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
